reasoning: The Lean encoding correctly captures $\frac{(X+2)^p - 2^p}{X}$: with $f = (X+2)^p$, the coefficients $\binom{p}{n} 2^{p-n}$ are all nonzero, so $f.\text{support} \setminus \{0\} = \{1,\ldots,p\}$, and the sum $\sum_{n=1}^{p} f.\text{coeff}(n) \cdot X^{n-1}$ equals $\sum_{n=1}^{p} \binom{p}{n} 2^{p-n} X^{n-1} = ((X+2)^p - 2^p)/X$. The hypothesis `p.Prime ∧ p > 2` for $p : \mathbb{N}$ is equivalent to "$p$ is an odd prime". `Irreducible` over `Polynomial ℤ` matches irreducibility in $\mathbb{Z}[X]$ (and since the polynomial has degree $p-1 \ge 2$, this coincides with the standard notion of irreducible polynomial). The quantifier structure and conclusion are as stated.
